description: Delivery delay: actual completion (OperationsResponse.actual_end_time) is later than the planned completion of the OperationsRequest/ProductionOrder.
$comment: Cross-source facet (CAPT-B Phase 2, building block 4 — CONTRACT, PARKED): OperationsResponse.actual_end_time (actual) later than OperationsRequest/ProductionOrder.planned_end_time (planned), joined on production_order_no. PARKED because the sim NEVER fills 'lieferdatum_ist' (→ actual_end_time) → the rule would find no actual value → would always stay 'unknown' (no raise). In practice the live order_overdue (single-entity constraint on CustomerOrder.due_date vs $now) already covers the same case. Contract + mapping are deliberately prepared here: once the sim delivers the actual date, remove 'parked' and the rule goes live — WITHOUT any code change. op=lte: HOLDS (ok) when actual_end_time <= planned_end_time; VIOLATED on overrun. Modeled as a multi_source class.
version_note: 2.1.0 (2026-07-12, CAPT-QTY) — parkedReason corrected: a SECOND, independent blocker was found.
parkedReason:
TWO independent blockers — BOTH must be cleared before this can go live.

(1) ORIGINAL: the sim never fills lieferdatum_ist (actual_end_time) → the rule finds no actual value → stays 'unknown' → never raises. In practice the live single-entity order_overdue (CustomerOrder.due_date vs $now) already covers the case.

(2) FOUND 2026-07-12 (CAPT-QTY): the GUARD IS ALSO BROKEN, identically to the retired qty_shortfall and the parked material_unavailable. `when` gates on SMProfile-OperationsResponse.status EQ 'offen', but /api/confirmations has no status column, so the source binds that attribute to the CONSTANT 'fertig'. 'fertig' === 'offen' is false for every row ⇒ the precondition is never met ⇒ the rule could never raise even if blocker (1) were fixed. Un-parking this rule after only fixing the sim's actual-date feed would produce a rule that still silently never fires. The literal 'offen' occurs in NO layer of the source (measured: erp.order_operations.status ∈ {OPEN,IN_PROCESS,PARTIAL_CONFIRMED,FINAL_CONFIRMED,CANCELLED}; erp.orders.status ∈ {CANCELLED,INVOICED,IN_PRODUCTION,WAITING_PARTS,ON_HOLD}; /api/orders German ∈ {storniert,abgeschlossen,in_arbeit,freigegeben}). Fixing it needs `when.rightAttr` support so the guard can gate on the ORDER's status instead of the confirmation's constant. ci/lint-cross-constraints.mjs now fails the build on any ACTIVE rule with such a guard, so this cannot be un-parked without fixing it.

(3) AND NOTE: planned_end_time (the promise this rule would test) is NOT trustworthy today — CAPT-ERP-TIME is repairing the ERP time axis (34.036 ops / 26 % end BEFORE their planned start, a backfill artifact). Build nothing on it until that lands.
